name: copilot-usage-tracker
description: Track GitHub Copilot prompts, effort, and efficiency inside Visual Studio Code.
author: Sabarish Kumar

commands.title: Copilot Usage Tracker: Ask With Tracking
Copilot Usage Tracker: Open Getting Started

walkthroughs.title: Copilot Usage Tracker
walkthroughs.description: Learn where to open the extension, how to enter a tracked prompt, and where to view reports inside Visual Studio Code.

steps.title: Open the Copilot Tracker view
steps.description:
Look at the left Activity Bar in VS Code and select **Copilot Tracker**. This opens the extension sidebar where you can see **Today**, **This Month**, and recent tracked prompts.

You can also open the Command Palette with `Ctrl+Shift+P` and run [Open Getting Started](command:copilotUsageTracker.openWalkthrough).

media.altText: Illustration showing the Copilot Tracker icon in the VS Code Activity Bar

steps.title: Create a tracked prompt
steps.description:
Open the **Copilot Tracker** container and choose the **Save Prompt** tab in the dashboard. Then enter:

1. Your prompt text
2. Planned effort in hours
3. Actual effort in hours

Select **Save Prompt** and the extension stores everything locally.

media.altText: Illustration showing the Ask With Tracking command and input boxes

steps.title: Generate reports and review usage
steps.description:
Open the **Report** tab in the dashboard. Choose a **From** date and **To** date, then select **Generate Report** to see total prompts, planned hours, actual hours, and efficiency saved.

Use **Export Excel** to create a modern `.xlsx` report file, or [Copilot Usage Tracker: Export JSON](command:copilotUsageTracker.exportJson) if you want a raw local export.

media.altText: Illustration showing the Reports view with date range fields and summary metrics

copilotUsageTracker.storageMode.description: Local storage backend used by the extension.

copilotUsageTracker.storePromptText.description: Store full prompt text locally. If disabled, only prompt metadata is stored.
